pageCaption.value: Indian tabla virtuoso Zakir Hussain and young American drummer Marcus Gilmore believe that creating music must be based on keeping the past but also exploring new possibilities.

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégé in Music, 2018-2019

text.value: Indian tabla virtuoso Zakir Hussain and young American drummer Marcus Gilmore believe that creating music must be based on keeping the past but also exploring new possibilities. Gilmore found himself, to his delight, performing with Hussain, and being encouraged to compose his first piece for orchestra.

pageTitle.value: Youssou N’Dour and Aurelio Martínez - Video

pageCaption.value: A year’s mentorship with Youssou N’Dour gives Aurelio Martínez, from Honduras, not only an opportunity to boost his musical skills and self-confidence in the company of “the icon of African music”, but also to learn more about the historic and musical background of his African ancestors as he visits N’Dour’s continent for the first time.

pageTitle.value: Sir Colin Davis and Josep Caballé Domenech - Video

pageCaption.value: One of the world’s greatest conductors, (the late) Sir Colin Davis believed that “you don’t control an orchestra, you are allowing things to happen.” Preparation and a deep cultural sense are what a conductor needs – lessons that Sir Colin instilled into his brilliant protégé, Josep Caballé Domenech, from Spain.

pageCaption.value: Convinced that the goal of architecture is to transform, British-Ghanaian architecture mentor Sir David Adjaye and his protégée Mariam Issoufou from Niger set about producing a concrete result for social good, with Issoufou designing a major cultural complex for her country’s capital, Niamey.

metadescription.value: Mentor David Adjaye and his protégée Mariam Issoufou are using architecture for social good in Niger’s capital. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: Dance is a wordless art form with few guidebooks, so for two choreographers − hip-hop dancer Khoudia Touré, from Senegal, and Canadian superstar choreographer Crystal Pite — to work face-to-face was a priceless experience.

metadescription.value: Dance is a wordless art form with few guidebooks, so for two choreographers − hip-hop dancer Khoudia Touré, from Senegal, and Canadian superstar choreographer Crystal Pite — to work face-to-face was a priceless experience.

pageCaption.value: Irish writer Colin Barrett recognizes that writing is a solitary pursuit, but also acknowledges the need to escape solitude and meet other people and fellow authors.
